concerns.text: T015 (correlation.py) is marked as requiring T014 (trends.py), but it actually requires the external metric data (GitHub stars/NPM downloads) which is not produced by T014. T014 only produces trend classifications. The correlation task needs a data-fetching task (e.g., T015a or a split of T015) that retrieves the external metrics before the correlation calculation can occur. Currently, T015 attempts to consume an artifact (external metrics) that does not exist in the dependency chain.

T015 description states it maps tags to GitHub/NPM repos but does not explicitly include the task of fetching the actual time-series data (stars/downloads) required for the correlation calculation defined in FR-007. The task conflates 'mapping' (finding the repo) with 'fetching' (getting the data). Without a distinct task to fetch the external time-series data, T015 cannot produce the correlation coefficient required by FR-007 and US-1.

T021 (decomposition.py) requires T013 (preprocess.py), which is correct. However, T021 implements the ADF test and differencing logic. T022 (residual independence check) is marked as requiring T021. This is a valid dependency. However, T023 (plots.py) requires T021 but not T022. While plots can be generated without the residual check, the spec (US-2) requires the plot to include 'Ljung-Box test results'. If T022 is the only place the Ljung-Box result is calculated/stored, T023 should depend on T022 to ensure the data is available for the plot generation.

T016 (bootstrapping) is marked as requiring T014 (trends.py). This is correct as it needs the slopes. However, T018 (generate trend_results.json) requires T016. T018 also aggregates 'trend and CI data'. T014 produces the trend data. T016 produces the CI data. The dependency chain T014 -> T016 -> T018 is valid. However, T015 (correlation) is also part of US1. If T015 produces the correlation data, T018 should arguably depend on T015 as well to ensure the final JSON includes the correlation results required by FR-007. Currently, T018 only depends on T016, potentially missing the correlation data in the final artifact.

T012 is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, it downloads the raw data dump. If T012 runs in parallel with T007 (generate_taxonomies) or T008 (setup data dirs), there is a risk of race conditions if T012 writes to `data/raw/` before T008 creates the directory structure. While T008 is in Phase 2 and T012 in Phase 3, the [P] tag on T012 implies it can run with other Phase 3 tasks. If T012 is run in parallel with T013 (preprocess), T013 might start before T012 finishes writing the file. The [P] tag on T012 is misleading if it implies independence from the file system state created by T008 or the completion of its own write operation relative to consumers.
